notes: OpenCode's --session chaining IS the session-resume mechanism: each `opencode run --session <id>` after the first joins the existing SQLite session row. Phases assert: (1) proc-PID pre-session born in ready, (2) ses_… session reconciled (new session_id), (3) turn 1 cycles ready→working→ready on the ses_… session, (4) process_exited fires on that ses_… session, (5) transcript_activity continues on the SAME ses_… session AFTER the exit — proving the SQLite row was reused. The same_session_as=handoff predicate on every post-handoff phase locks the assertion to the SQLite-backed session id; the state cycles cannot be re-matched by the proc-PID pre-session.

text: SQLite session row born (different session_id than session_birth)
First user prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
First turn completes back to ready on the same ses_… id
Process exits on the ses_… session
transcript_activity fires on the SAME ses_… session AFTER the process exited — the second `opencode run --session` joined the existing SQLite session
